event: Set design for the opera The Tsar's Bride
description: A theatrical set design showing an ornate Russian interior — a terem or royal chamber. The walls are decorated with religious frescoes including tall figures of saints and an archangel with wings on the right side. The architectural details include pointed arches, ornamental columns, and a domed ceiling with golden patterns. Striped curtains frame the sides of the stage. Small standing figures appear at the bottom of the scene. The overall style combines Russian medieval architectural motifs with vivid coloring in reds, golds, and deep blues.

_meta.title: Sketch for the opera "The Tsar's Bride" by Nikolai Rimsky-Korsakov
